verdict_reasoning: HSPD1 encodes HSP60, a mitochondrial matrix chaperone canonically localized to the mitochondrial inner compartment. However, substantial literature documents non-baseline surface display: (1) cancer-cell ecto-HSP60 on tumor cells and tumor-derived exosomes, with documented binding to cell-surface TLR4 / CD36, and anti-HSP60 antibody and CAR-T programs targeting tumor-cell surface HSP60; (2) stress-induced surface translocation during heat shock, oxidative stress, and ER stress in multiple cell types; (3) cell-death-related externalization during apoptosis and necroptosis. The surface pool is real, documented by flow cytometry and immunofluorescence on intact cells, and is the target of active therapeutic programs. Baseline compartment is mitochondrial matrix (inner-leaflet facing), but the documented stress / cancer / cell-death ecto-pool qualifies as cell_state_induced contextual surface accessibility.
